verses.text: Men know how to mine silver, refine gold, and sink shafts into the dark earth far from any dweller. Human skill probes the hidden places of the world. Our ingenuity in seeking treasure is remarkable — and yet it cannot reach the highest prize.

Men know how to mine silver, refine gold, and sink shafts into the dark earth far from any dweller. Human skill probes the hidden places of the world. Our ingenuity in seeking treasure is remarkable — and yet it cannot reach the highest prize.

Men know how to mine silver, refine gold, and sink shafts into the dark earth far from any dweller. Human skill probes the hidden places of the world. Our ingenuity in seeking treasure is remarkable — and yet it cannot reach the highest prize.

The earth yields bread above while fire and gems lie below, in paths no bird of prey has spied nor lion trodden. Man uncovers what no creature can find. Yet the cleverness that wins jewels still falls short of true wisdom.

The earth yields bread above while fire and gems lie below, in paths no bird of prey has spied nor lion trodden. Man uncovers what no creature can find. Yet the cleverness that wins jewels still falls short of true wisdom.
He splits the rock, cuts channels through it, and dams the streams to bring hidden things to light. Nothing in the ground escapes his search. But the deepest wisdom is not won by digging or strength.
He splits the rock, cuts channels through it, and dams the streams to bring hidden things to light. Nothing in the ground escapes his search. But the deepest wisdom is not won by digging or strength.
He splits the rock, cuts channels through it, and dams the streams to bring hidden things to light. Nothing in the ground escapes his search. But the deepest wisdom is not won by digging or strength.

'But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?' The deep and the sea answer, 'It is not in me.' Wisdom is not a buried ore. The thing man most needs cannot be quarried from the earth.

'But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?' The deep and the sea answer, 'It is not in me.' Wisdom is not a buried ore. The thing man most needs cannot be quarried from the earth.

'But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?' The deep and the sea answer, 'It is not in me.' Wisdom is not a buried ore. The thing man most needs cannot be quarried from the earth.

It cannot be bought with gold or silver, nor weighed against onyx, sapphire, crystal, coral, or rubies; no jewel can equal it. True wisdom is beyond all price. The world's most precious things cannot purchase what only God can give.

It cannot be bought with gold or silver, nor weighed against onyx, sapphire, crystal, coral, or rubies; no jewel can equal it. True wisdom is beyond all price. The world's most precious things cannot purchase what only God can give.

Again the question: whence comes wisdom? It is hidden from every living eye, and even death has only heard its rumour. No creature can locate it. Wisdom dwells beyond the reach of all that man can search.

Again the question: whence comes wisdom? It is hidden from every living eye, and even death has only heard its rumour. No creature can locate it. Wisdom dwells beyond the reach of all that man can search.

God alone understands its way, for He sees the ends of the earth, weighs the wind, and measures the waters. The Maker of all knows where wisdom lives. He who ordered the world holds the key to its meaning.

God alone understands its way, for He sees the ends of the earth, weighs the wind, and measures the waters. The Maker of all knows where wisdom lives. He who ordered the world holds the key to its meaning.

Having searched it out, God tells man where it is found: 'the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom; and to depart from evil is understanding.' The highest knowledge is within reach of the humble. To revere God and shun evil is the wisdom the whole earth cannot buy.
